meta.default_title: PingCast — branded status pages for SaaS
meta.default_description: Open-source alternative to Atlassian Statuspage. Uptime monitoring + incident timelines + email subscribers + custom domains. From $9/mo, self-host MIT-free.

common.error_generic: Something went wrong. Try again in a moment.

footer.newsletter_heading: Get 1-2 emails a month from a solo dev
footer.newsletter_sub: New blog posts on status pages + indie SaaS, product updates, occasional honest lessons from shipping. No marketing fluff.
footer.newsletter_helper: 1-2 emails a month. Unsubscribe in one click.
footer.tagline: PingCast — branded status pages for SaaS, at a third of Atlassian's price. Open source under MIT.

links.how_to_create: How to create a status page

landing.hero_headline: Branded status pages for indie SaaS
landing.hero_sub_em: at a third of Atlassian's price.
landing.hero_sub: Custom domain, incident timeline, email subscribers, uptime monitoring built in. $9/mo founder price (first 100 customers locked forever), $19/mo retail. Or self-host MIT-free.
landing.hero_microcopy: No credit card · 5-min setup · Free forever for under-5-monitor setups
landing.features_heading: Everything Atlassian Statuspage has, none of the bloat
landing.features_sub: Built for indie SaaS, not enterprise. Same power, no procurement-process pricing.
landing.feature_status_page_body: Custom domain, your logo + accent colour, incident timeline, email subscribers with double opt-in.
landing.feature_uptime_body: HTTP / TCP / DNS / ping checks, configurable interval and alert threshold, 90-day history retention.
landing.feature_atlassian_body: Paste your Atlassian Statuspage JSON export — monitors, incidents, and full timeline carry over verbatim.
landing.feature_widget_body: Drop a banner into your app on incidents, or a status badge into your README. Both updated live.
landing.feature_self_host_body: Run the same binaries we run, on your own infra. docker-compose up, 5 minutes to live.
landing.feature_pro_body: Custom domains, monitor groups, maintenance windows, manual incidents, CSV export. First 100 customers lock $9/mo forever.
landing.social_proof_heading: Built in public, paid for by users
landing.social_proof_sub: We don't take VC money. We take subscription money. That keeps the priorities aligned.
landing.cta_heading: Ship your status page this afternoon
landing.cta_sub: 10 minutes from signup to a public status page at status.yourcompany.com.

pricing.page_subtitle: Founder's price for the first 100 customers — $9/mo locked forever. Retail $19/mo after that. Self-host MIT-free, always.
pricing.pro_subtitle: Branded status pages for paying customers
pro_features: CSV export, RSS feed, SVG badge
pricing.self_host_subtitle: MIT-licensed, run it on your own infra

faq.q: What happens after the founder cap?
faq.a: Founder customers keep $9/mo forever. New signups land on the $19 retail price.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Atlassian Statuspage?
faq.a: Yes. Paste your JSON export at /import/atlassian and we re-create monitors, incidents and the full timeline in one transaction. 60 seconds.

faq.q: Is there a free trial of Pro?
faq.a: Free tier is the trial — same checker, public status page (with our badge). Upgrade once you need branding or custom domain.
Audience carve-outs (different notifications to different customer tiers) — on the roadmap. SLA reports with contractual math — stay on Atlassian if you need them.

auth.register_sub: Free tier is permanent. Upgrade to Pro any time for branded status pages and custom domains.
auth.register_slug_help: Becomes pingcast.io/status/your-slug. Letters, numbers and hyphens, 3–30 characters.

errors.duplicate_email: An account with this email already exists.
errors.duplicate_slug: This slug is taken. Pick another.
errors.weak_password: Password too short — at least 8 characters.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.monitors_empty: No monitors yet. Add your first one to start checking uptime.
dashboard.getting_started_sub: A few quick steps to wire up your first alert. This goes away once you're fully set up.
dashboard.getting_started_step_2: Add a notification channel (Telegram or webhook)
dashboard.getting_started_step_3: Bind the channel to your monitor so alerts reach you

monitors.delete_confirm: Delete this monitor and all its history?

channels.title: Notification channels
channels.empty: No channels yet. Add Telegram or a webhook to start getting alerts.

api_keys.title: API keys
api_keys.sub: API keys let other tools push to and read from PingCast on your behalf. Treat them like passwords.

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Notes on building PingCast. Shipped in public, so the pivots, the pricing experiments, and the occasional 3-AM incident are all here.
blog.subscribe_sub: New posts + honest build-in-public updates from a solo dev. No marketing, no pitch decks, just what worked and what didn't.
